MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES
Off Calendar May-25-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant's motion to compel further responses to form and special interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part.

50.1 - 50.6:
The Court is unable to determine the sufficiency of the responses in light of the supplemental answers of April 1, 2005. The parties are ordered to meet and confer.

1 - 5:
Granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. The answers are evasive. If plaintiff is unable to provide a particular date in response to interrogatory #5, the response shall comply with CCP Section 2030(f)(1)

9:
Granted. If there is no further agreement other than the contract alleged in paragraph 9 of the complaint, plaintiff shall verify that representation. If there are additional agreements, plaintiff is ordered to respond separately without referring to a response to other interrogatories.

13 - 15:
Granted. The objections pursuant to CCP Section 2030(c)(5) are overruled.

16:
Granted. The objection is overruled and stricken. The term non-monetary contribution is not wholly unintelligible.

22 - 26:
The Court is unable to determine the sufficiency of the responses and whether the interrogatories have been withdrawn in light of the supplemental interrogatories (#30 - 40) propounded by defendant. The Court makes no rulings as to the sufficiency of those responses. The parties are ordered to meet and confer.

27, 28:
Denied. The request for all personal income for the past ten years and all bank accounts is overbroad.

29:
Granted. The objection pursuant to CCP Section 2030(c)(5) is overruled.

For those interrogatories where a further answer has been ordered, Plaintiff is ordered to provide supplemental responses within 15 days of notice of this order. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion & Ccp 1008 Motion To Reconsider Prior Order To Disqualify Counsel For Purported Conflict Of Interest And Ccp 473 Motion For Mandatory Relief Based On Mistake And/Or Excusable Neglect
Off Calendar Nov-03-2004 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Philip S. Ward, Esq., HASSARD BONNINGTON, Two Embarcadero Center, Suiter 1800, San francisco, CA 94111-3993, 415-288-9800, specially appearing on behalf of moving party David Mattingly, Esq., and plaintiff and moving party Jeffrey S. Goins. David J. Mattingly, Esq., Law Offices of David J. Mattingly, 368 Hayes Street, San Francisco, CA 94102-4411, 415-551-0882, on behalf of plaintiff and moving party Jeffrey Goins. Jeffrey G. Gibson, Esq., GOLDSTEIN, GELLMAN, MELBOSTAD, GIBSON & HARRIS, 1388 Sutter Street, Suite 1000, San Franicsco, CA 94109-5494, 415-673-5600, on behalf of defendant and opposing party Kenneth Dean.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling and DENIED the CCP 1008 Motion for Reconsideration. After argument the Court GRANTED the CCP 473 Motion for Mandatory Relief based on mistake and/or excusable neglect. The issue of disqualification of counsel was taken under submission. If further hearing is required such hearing shall be set for October 26, 2004. Briefing for the hearing, if necessary, shall be due on October 19, 2004. Honorable Ronald Evans Quidachay. Reported by Beldon Yee (302/JEN)
